server.name: Sandbox MCP
server.summary: General-purpose Python execution sandbox for MCP clients, with persistent execution, artifact capture, Manim rendering, guarded shell access, and lightweight web app workflows.
server.description: Sandbox MCP gives language models a guarded Python workspace with persistent execution state, shell access with security filtering, artifact management, Manim rendering, package installation, and small web app workflows. It is designed to stay broadly useful for experimentation, teaching, demos, analysis, and prototyping rather than being tied to one marketplace or one domain.

best_for: creating plots, images, tables, and file artifacts
launching or exporting small Flask and Streamlit demos
not_for: untrusted production workloads without an outer container or VM

notes: Place the HTTP server behind TLS termination and authentication before exposing it remotely.
Treat the sandbox as a guarded environment, not as a hardened isolation boundary.
Use reverse proxy controls, network policy, and outer-container isolation for public hosting.

tools.name: execute
tools.description: Run Python code in the sandbox with persistent state, artifact capture, and optional web app launch.

tools.name: list_artifacts
tools.description: List files generated by sandbox runs, including plots, images, animations, and exports.

tools.name: clear_cache
tools.description: Clear cached compilations and related execution state to force a fresh run path.

tools.name: cleanup_artifacts
tools.description: Remove current artifacts and stop any tracked web app processes created by the sandbox.

tools.name: start_repl
tools.description: Start the basic interactive Python REPL workflow exposed by the sandbox.

tools.name: start_web_app
tools.description: Launch a small Flask or Streamlit app from code and return its local access URL.

tools.name: cleanup_temp_artifacts
tools.description: Delete old temporary sandbox artifact directories older than the requested age threshold.

tools.name: shell_execute
tools.description: Run a shell command inside the guarded environment with security checks and a timeout.

tools.name: create_manim_animation
tools.description: Render a Manim Community animation from provided scene code and return output metadata.

tools.name: list_manim_animations
tools.description: List Manim renders created by the sandbox and their saved artifact locations.

tools.name: cleanup_manim_animation
tools.description: Delete one saved Manim render by its animation identifier.

tools.name: get_manim_examples
tools.description: Return small Manim example scenes that can be adapted into new animations immediately.

tools.name: get_execution_info
tools.description: Inspect the current sandbox environment, including paths, virtualenv status, and tracked globals.

tools.name: get_artifact_report
tools.description: Summarize current artifacts with category counts and persistent execution context details.

tools.name: categorize_artifacts
tools.description: Group current artifacts into higher-level categories such as plots, images, data, and videos.

tools.name: cleanup_artifacts_by_type
tools.description: Delete artifacts from one category without clearing the rest of the sandbox outputs.

tools.name: start_enhanced_repl
tools.description: Start the enhanced REPL workflow with helper commands for artifacts, packages, and Manim.

tools.name: execute_with_artifacts
tools.description: Run Python code while explicitly tracking resulting artifacts and execution metadata.

tools.name: backup_current_artifacts
tools.description: Create a named backup snapshot of the current artifact set for rollback or comparison.

tools.name: list_artifact_backups
tools.description: List saved artifact backups with sizes, timestamps, and file counts.

tools.name: rollback_to_backup
tools.description: Restore artifacts from a saved backup snapshot into the current sandbox workspace.

tools.name: get_backup_details
tools.description: Inspect one artifact backup in detail, including categories and contained files.

tools.name: cleanup_old_backups
tools.description: Prune older artifact backups and keep only the requested number of recent snapshots.

tools.name: export_web_app
tools.description: Package a Flask or Streamlit app into an exportable project with deployment assets.

tools.name: list_web_app_exports
tools.description: List previously exported web app bundles and their generated deployment files.

tools.name: get_export_details
tools.description: Inspect one exported web app bundle, including files, metadata, and status.

tools.name: build_docker_image
tools.description: Build a Docker image from an exported web app bundle for local or hosted deployment.

tools.name: cleanup_web_app_export
tools.description: Delete one exported web app bundle and its generated deployment assets.

tools.name: install_package
tools.description: Install a Python package into the sandbox environment, optionally pinning a version.

tools.name: list_installed_packages
tools.description: List the Python packages currently available to sandbox executions.

tools.name: get_sandbox_limitations
tools.description: Explain sandbox constraints, available subsystems, and recommended workarounds.

tools.name: get_comprehensive_help
tools.description: Return a structured usage guide covering execution, artifacts, packages, web apps, and Manim.

prompts.name: manim_storyboard_skill
prompts.description: Turns a concept into a storyboard, a renderable Manim scene, and a suggested sandbox workflow.

prompts.name: manim_scene_template
prompts.description: Generate a single self-contained Manim scene from a concept, duration target, and quality preset.

prompts.name: sandbox_example_template
prompts.description: Generate a compact sandbox demo that creates an artifact such as a plot, image, table, or data file.

prompts.name: sandbox_web_app_template
prompts.description: Generate a small Flask or Streamlit demo that is ready for launch or export from the sandbox.

resources.description: Structured summary of the server, its capabilities, and recommended usage patterns.
Machine-readable overview of tools, prompts, resources, skills, and templates exposed by Sandbox MCP.
Machine-readable list of higher-level skills available from the sandbox server.
Machine-readable list of user-activated interactive templates exposed as MCP prompts.

marketplace_notes.generic_applicability: The server stays general-purpose and useful outside any single catalog, while still surfacing richer metadata for MCPHub, MCPMarket, LobeHub, and similar directories.
marketplace_notes.hosted_listing_readiness: Remote hosting should point to a protected Streamable HTTP deployment and include authentication, TLS, and an outer isolation boundary.
